title: Phase two roadmap: content density, light progression, then living-world slices
summary: Thirteenth structured packet for Eco Explorer. The project has crossed out of foundation cleanup and into deliberate phase-two planning. The live five-biome world is now denser, the first light journal-first progression layer is in place, and the two immediate follow-up fixes both landed cleanly: shared species now respect local sightings for per-biome progress, and dense journal pages now stay navigable through compact windowing instead of a dead-end cutoff.

execution_order.goal: Expand authored discovery density across the live five-biome chain.
execution_order.why_now: This is the highest payoff next move because it makes the game feel fuller without changing the core interaction model.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a compact lightweight-progression handoff while the content pass is underway.
execution_order.why_now: This can run in parallel so the next system step is concrete before the content-density pass finishes.

execution_order.goal: Review the content-density pass for science safety, readability, and biome identity.
execution_order.why_now: More content helps only if the new entries still read clearly and keep each habitat distinct.

execution_order.goal: Implement one calm journal-first progression layer using per-biome survey states.
execution_order.why_now: After the content pass lands, this is the smallest next system that can improve motivation without breaking the cozy direction.

execution_order.goal: Review the first progression layer for tone, readability, and learning fit.
execution_order.why_now: Progression can drift into quest or score energy quickly, so a critique pass should check tone before living-world systems open up.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate for every new content entry.
New entries should deepen biome identity instead of making habitats blur together.
The journal should stay calm and notebook-like, not dashboard-like.
Progression should reward noticing and revisiting, not add chores or urgency.
Prefer compact per-biome survey states over quest logs, score ranks, or another prose-heavy journal card.
Do not activate the broad parked living-world pack until content density and light progression are both stable.
Keep the build green after each step.
acceptance_focus: Each live biome feels denser and more worth exploring.
The next progression layer stays cozy and discovery-first.
The first progression pass uses one compact per-biome survey state instead of a checklist or global score ladder.
Shared species do not inflate later-biome survey progress before the player actually sees them there.
The denser journal remains fully navigable without losing its calm notebook feel.
The game earns larger phase-two systems instead of jumping to them early.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-post-28-roadmap.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-lightweight-progression-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as the phase-two sequence after the coastal science cleanup, and implement the lightweight progression step as compact per-biome survey states rather than a quest or score system.
